title: what the pebble should have been
text:
final update:
it finally broke. after 2yrs of heavy everyday use.
i loved this watch. absolutely recommend.
the battery life was outstanding my entire 2yrs of wearing it. easily a full month of use on each charge.
below is a detailed review of my life with this watch:

====
day 1)
this watch is everything i wished the pebble watch was. ridiculous battery life, always-on screen, nice-looking (looks like the Apple watch, i been told so by Apple-watch owner), heart rate, GPS, pedometer, sleep tracking, thousands of watch faces, nice fitness tracking info in the app, and syncs with Google fit too

the only negative is that the contrast of the display isn't as sharp as it is on my Garmin vivosmart, but this may just be what happens with color e-ink displays. mind you, it's still very easily visible, plus has the backlight in case you need to see it in the dark, but it's just not quiet as clear as a black+white e-ink.

gps and heart

title: Inconsistent and innacurate GPS
text:
I really wanted to like this watch. I trail run ultra marathon distances and have been looking for an inexpensive GPS watch to track my workouts and races. Comparisons were tested against Apple Watch Series 2, iPhone 7, and Garmin 935.

Pros:
- Battery life is legitimately great.
- Integration with iOS seemed great.
- Heart rate tracking seemed accurate, by comparison.
- Screen was always easy for me to read.

Cons:
- Inconsistent GPS tracking: Over several runs of the same 4 mile trail route my distance would be anywhere from .5 mile short to an entire mile too long.
-- On a 20 mile trail run my distance was .5 mile short, but elevation recorded was 2k ft more than reality.
- Inconsistent activity information synced between watch and iPhone app: On that same run my watch activity read 19.5 miles, but the MiFit app read 19 miles. ??!! When I tried to export to AmazTools the .gpx file only shows 12.7 miles. :/

Conclusion:

title: Way better than I expected.
text:
[Updates to the review in regards to Battery Life, Steps Counter and Sleep Tracker]
I have to admit that my expectations for the Amazfit Bip were quite low, especially after using a Gear S3 Frontier watch for the past six months. But I really wanted to escape having to charge my watch every three or four days, and if I forgot to charge my watch on Day 4, I could not make it all through the workday on Day 5. The Amazfit Bip's promise of 6 to 10 times the battery life of my previous smartwatch enticed me to give it a try.

The Amazfit Bip has almost everything the average user needs from a smartwatch, and very little of what they don't.

PROS:
* Screen: Yes, it isn't a flashy AMOLED screen, but it is always on and perfectly readable outdoors. There's also a good backlight for use in darker locations. I take functionality over appearance any day, and the Amazfit Bip delivers in that department.
* Lightweight: This watch doesn't feel like it's slowing you

title: I'd give it more than 1 star if it came ...
text: I'd give it more than 1 star if it came with a manual) instructions on how to set it up so that I could use it. Sadly, it didn't and it's going back because I shouldn't have to Google random forums to find out what to do.

title: Just what I've been looking for
text: Hello smart watch companies, THIS is what you should be making!  I can't understand today's smartwatches,; super expensive, hardly on at all unless you move your wrist 'just right' and you have to charge it every night.  This one has none of that. On all the time (reflective LCD), less than $100, and as I write this it's been 2 weeks since I charged it last and it's still at 78 percent charge.  Even has a compass mode!  Downsides?  Minor in my opinion but here goes.  Watch band is a little too short (and I have a small wrist), the reflective display is hard to see in the dark, when you activate the backlight it washes out the colors.  One thing about setup, be patient.  Took me a bit to get it going had to remove other Bluetooth connections on my phone, but put them back afterward and all is fine. Overall I couldn't be happier with this watch.  Oh and you'll need to download a couple apps to get other watchfaces.

title: Great for the price
text: No way to compare data collected, but sleep statistics seem reasonably accurate, steps are a bit off because I talk with hands, and GPS is a bit slow to engage. Had to download a watch face since provided ones are either too small and crowded to read in daylight, have too little information and force you to swipe for usable data, or are simply hard to read due to dainty graphics. Considering the discounted price I paid, I am satisfied. Had a competing, more expensive product performed in a similar way I would likely be far less satisfied. For the price it's fine.

title: The watch is great; the app is not.
text:
The watch is good, guys, but the app is not. It’s not user-friendly. If you do any kind of workouts other than running or cycling (indoor or outdoor), there is no way to add that to your app. Since the MiFit app isn’t compatible with ANY other app, this forces you to log everything in two different apps every time. Just save up the price of this watch for two months and buy a Fitbit or a Garmin. You’ll be MUCH happier.

UPDATE: after I returned my Bip and got my money back, I put it toward getting a Garmin Vivoactice 3, and friends, the Garmin app is SO much better than MiiFit. Just get a Garmin or a Fitbit. You won’t regret it. I’ve had my Garmin for about a week now, and I am so much happier and less frustrated with my fitness tracking 👌🏿

title: Not so impressed anymore
text:
UPDATE: Less than a year into it, the watch developed a glitch, where only parts of the screen are being redrawn. Not sure if it's a faulty hardware, or if a software update broke it, but it had become unusable. The support has not been helpful: suggested full reset, which I performed without any improvement. Dropping from 5 stars to 1.

After a few years with an Android Wear watch, I was skeptical of a $67 watch with a proprietary OS being any better. Yet, five days in, it had been rock solid. Notifications arrive immediately, battery, while not holding as much as advertised (I suspect because of a custom watch face I'm using) is still far superior to anything else out there. It's light and comfortable.

The only thing that I wish was customizable is the font for notifications.

title: Think Twice or more  before buying
text:
I have had this watch since 6/2019 using it off and on
I have using this for the last 240 days straight

Here are some of my issues
It seems to be always updating the A-GPS (especially when i am on the treadmill)
When it is updating, it is not keeping track of your steps
There has been many times I thought I was losing steps and this is at least one of the issues
I cannot find anywhere in the app to change the update schedule
I have consistently walked the 10 thousand steps everyday and there was the one day it could not connect to the server so it recorded only 8 thousand steps for that one day and broke my record
I know that I make sure I walk the 10 thousand steps each and every day and see that it shows

5/16 update

battery is dying - in 5 to 6 days it has gone from 100% to 12%
(it usually lasted a good 23 days in the beginning plus I have turned off most of the features as I do not use them)

5/9/2020

title: How do I get a large instruction manual?
text: I can't read the tiny instructions

title: Died after a whole two months.
text: Works good when new, the best thing about this watch is the battery life. You will not be able to remember when the last time you charged it was. GPS works well, the biggest draw back is that it did not last more than two months for me, and it's not like I took it to mount everest or anything. Just took it to work and home. Then two months later, dead. I will be looking for a refund.

title: Spend the money and get a better watch
text: The battery life outstanding!  The step counter isn't even close, nor the blm.  I bought this for my husband and I was very disappointed.

title: Battery life
text: Battery life is not good as promised.

title: Good but screen is not great
text: Battery life was great but screen resolution was not good. Brightness is below par.

title: Great watch for the price
text:
I've been thinking of getting a smartwatch for a couple years now, but given the limited options, high price of many of them, and the lack of any real need for it I couldn't justify it. Then I saw this for about $80 and figured I'd give it a try. Very happy I did. Been using about a week now and definitely happy with the purchase:

Pros:
Easy connectivity
Amazing battery life
Lightweight and comfortable
Fully configurable in terms of which alerts you get on the watch
Seemingly accurate step and HR tracking
Easy connection to Google Fit
Price!

Cons:
Wish it had a built in music player/bluetooth connectivity for headphones so I could leave the phone home on runs
Alerts are one way only. Some watches give ability to take some limited set of actions, but this does not.
The option in the app to suppress certain sections on the watch is not working. Specifically want to remove the Alipay function since I do not use it.

title: Good quality
text: Beautiful watch , my brother loved

title: Great Features and Poor Display
text: The features thus far are great! Appeared to track my sleeping time accurately. The negatives are a lousy screen looks like an old television projection screen and low watch strap quality. Can't recommend this watch unless it sold for 1/2 the current $80 price tag.

title: great battery
text: the battery last about 2weeks on average.  My wife has changed the watch face and always has tracking on.  She uses it on runs and workouts.  It's a nice paired watch with paired down features so you don't have to charge every other day

title: Piece of crap.
text: Piece of crap. This worked for a few months and then quit.

title: Durable easy to use
text: Bought this watch for our 14 year old son who wanted to do tracking of steps swimming and other outdoor things. He is not the easiest person on items and the Amazon  bip  Has handled His busy life

title: Incredible value
text:
I have only had this for a week but I am very impressed with the functionality for the price and the battery life is really outstanding. I downloaded a watch face with a seconds display and I turned the heart rate mothering on all the time - both of which are supposed to really suck the battery down and I still had about half the battery at the end of the week.

There are a huge number of watch faces available out there now including those with US displays. Downloading 3rd party watch faces into the watch could be a little simpler if it had support in their app but there are plenty of other apps out there that support the 3rd party displays. I was confused at 1st thinking that I needed to find one with a display of temperature in Fahrenheit but found that that's actually a setting in the watch.

The app settings for mothering exercise and heart rate have really been interesting as is the analysis of my sleep. Quite the deal for the price- loving it.

title: Great performance at a great price. Fantastic battery life !
text: Works great and the battery lasts for weeks! Download another app for additional watch faces  - I use Amaztools. No complaints whatsoever
